items.title: 'No sign of danger': Friend details horror of fatal shark attack

children.content: The best friend of a Perth father killed by a shark off Rottnest Island says there was "absolutely no warning" before the attack.

items.title: What is 'pink-slime' journalism and has it infiltrated Australian media?

image.altText: A hand holding a phone wiht the bunbury guardian home page displayed on the screen with a pink background ebhind the phone

children.content: Experts say a series of AI-generated digital news outlets is the first recorded example of "pink-slime" journalism in Australia, with the website owner claiming the now-deleted outlets were an experiment gone wrong.

items.title: Premier's fracking warning over possible gas shortfall sparks outrage

children.content: Roger Cook raises the prospect of gas fracking in WA's Kimberley region, warning the state could face future energy shortfalls if Woodside Energy's massive Browse project does not proceed.

gallery.altText: Roger Cook speaks at a press conference while wearing a high vis vest.
A close-up shot of protesters outside Parliament House in Perth holding signs saying 'Save Scott Reef' and opposing fracking.
A crowd of protesters outside Parliament House in Perth holding signs saying 'Save Scott Reef' and opposing fracking.

items.title: What's going wrong inside the City of Perth?

image.altText: Bruce Reynolds gives a subtle, toothless smile to the camera in front of council buildings.

avatar.alt: A blonde woman named Courtney Withers stands wearing a blazer looking serious.

children.content: It's been labelled "dysfunctional" and "diabolical". What does the lord mayor have to say about the embattled City of Perth?

items.title: Toxic mouse bait forces school to shut for 'deep clean' in plague-hit town

children.content: Students of the Western Australian School of Agriculture in the WA town of Morawa are sent home and the school is closed after toxic mouse bait intended for paddocks is spread on the campus.

articles.title: A mouse plague has broken out. Here's what you need to know

children.content: Mice in their millions are eating grain crops, destroying food, and invading houses. Here's how it all started.

items.title: Freo notches 10th straight win with Saints triumph, Tigers down Bombers

children.content: Fremantle makes club history with a 30-point victory over St Kilda, as Essendon loses three key players to injury in an 18-point loss to Richmond.

children.content: One of Australian basketball's most successful coaches and one of the national league's greatest players have paired in Adelaide.

items.title: Perth almost created a City of South Park in 2014 — here's why it might again

children.content: In 2015 the Barnett government abandoned plans to merge Perth councils — but now the idea has reared its head again. Here's what you need to know.

articles.title: 'I'm over it': Minister lashes City of Perth over 'diabolical' culture

children.content: The "diabolical" City of Perth should be merged with its neighbouring council, a WA government minister has claimed, describing the "pettiness, infighting, immaturity" by elected members as "galling."

bag of lettuce.

items.title: There are 40,000 trusts linked to Australian farms. Here's why

children.content: Despite winning a carve-out to changes to family trusts announced in the budget, farmers are still worried about what tax reform means for them.

items.title: Last private midwife north of Perth to close as costs of business rise

children.content: Soon-to-be parents in regional WA will have less choice over where and how they give birth as care options diminish, midwives say.

items.title: Diphtheria outbreak could be 'contained within months'

children.content: Rapid vaccination could stem the spread of the potentially deadly diphtheria outbreak, but the government's response has been criticised as being too slow.

image.altText: A woman holding up her t-shirt sleeve, revealing a small band-aid over the spot where she was vaccinated.

items.title: Global mental health disorder cases double in three decades

children.content: A surge over the last three decades means mental disorders now account for more than 17 per cent of all disability worldwide, according to a report published this week.

items.title: As coral reefs are wiped out from bleaching, this one brings hope

children.content: In the midst of a mass coral bleaching event along the West Australian coast, a group of scientists have discovered a section of reef that has been able to withstand the heat.

items.title: WA gas users warn Labor not to repeat flaws in state reservation scheme

children.content: Western Australian gas users are urging the Albanese government to build stronger transparency and enforcement into its proposed national gas reserve scheme.

items.title: Chalmers asked to explain how he 'landed on 30 per cent' tax rate

children.content: The Productivity Commission was not asked to model how changes to capital gains tax would impact Australia's economy. Business groups say Treasurer Jim Chalmers must explain how the government concluded its tax changes would ultimately be a boon to productivity.

items.title: Inside this town, 'it's not a secret' that half the people don't officially exist

children.content: In a community like no other, what happens when people aren't counted? Welcome to Robinvale, where these workers and their families don't exist.

items.title: Family secrets and scones highlight Australia's leading cause of death

children.content: Gwen Knox has lost her grandmother, aunt, brother and sister to dementia. The West Australian woman has now written a play that she hopes starts important conversations about dementia and truth-telling.
